dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can.
instructions.note: Solution = the folder this file sits in. Table / object name = this file's name. Not to be confused with SolutionManagement, which tracks the files of solutions created on disk by the Creation Page — this model is the planning-level Solution a developer creates in the Develop tab and links to a Product.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: A Solution created in the Develop tab — linked to a Product and filled in from the solution-spec questions (what kind of solution, its purpose, hosting, language, frameworks).

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data.

entries.rules: not empty; unique within its Product

entries.description: The Product this Solution belongs to

entries.rules: one of: website / api / database / application
entries.description: What kind of solution it is (same list as the framework's solution spec)

entries.rules: valid JSON keyed by solution-spec question ids (purpose, hosting, language, frameworks)

entries.description: Whether we created this Solution's repository or imported (linked) an existing one

entries.rules: the repository's web URL when one is linked
entries.description: The GitHub repository linked to this Solution

entries.rules: one of: private / public; unknown (null) for imported repos
entries.description: Whether the linked repository is private or public

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Deleted with its Product, or when the user removes the Solution.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields that need extra protection?
sensitiveFields.answer: No. The GitHub repository URL is not sensitive; the GitHub token that creates repositories is never stored in this table — it lives in the OS credential store, like the AI provider keys.

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.answer: A Solution always belongs to an existing Product; solutionType stays in its list; answers always parses as JSON; origin is 'created' or 'imported', and githubVisibility (when set) is 'private' or 'public'.

changesOverTime.guidance: Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change.

rounds.change: Add origin (created / imported), githubUrl, and githubVisibility so a Solution can link an existing GitHub repository or record one created through the GitHub API as private or public.
